data.title: Italy said to lift taxes on capital gains from bitcoin to 42% from 26%
data.description:
Deputy Finance Minister Maurizio Leo said Wednesday that Italy plans to hike capital gains tax on cryptocurrencies like bitcoin (BTC-USD) to 42% from 26%, according to several media reports.

The price of bitcoin (BTC-USD) appeared unfazed by the news, having risen 2.4% to $67.6K in late morning trading. The token is up some 53% so far this year.

Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni’s cabinet took this action as the “phenomenon is spreading,” Leo said of bitcoin (BTC-USD) during a conference call, Bloomberg reported.

The decision aligns with Italy's plans to bolster its digital services tax to boost revenue for the government's 2025 budget.

But previous attempts by other countries to tax crypto trading have not always been a boon for government budgets.

In 2022, for example, India imposed a tax on income from crypto assets and non-fungible tokens. That prompted investors to transition to offshore platforms to circumvent the tax, a move that pulled down trading volumes in India, Bloomberg reported.

data.title: Vestas rises on pricing pact by Chinese rivals; wind turbine catches fire in Denmark
data.description:
Vestas Wind (OTCPK:VWDRY) +2.6% in Wednesday's trading following news that several of China's biggest wind turbine manufacturers signed an agreement seeking to stop the price war in the wind turbine market.

Fear of competition from China has contributed to pressure on Vestas' (OTCPK:VWDRY) share price, which has fallen 40% YTD.

Separately, Vestas (OTCPK:VWDRY) said a wind turbine at Bjerringbro in western Denmark that was produced by the company had caught fire, but no one was injured and the rest of its fleet was working as normal.

The company said the cause of the fire was not yet known; local authorities said it was not possible to extinguish the flames and that the turbine would be allowed to burn out on its own.

data.title: Amazon releases new Kindles, including first color, AI models, ahead of holidays
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) is releasing its first new Kindle models in two years, including its first color and artificial intelligence-powered e-readers, the company announced today.

The new Kindle Colorsoft provides the same high contrast, fast page turning, auto-adjusting light and long battery life as other Kindles, but now adds color to images. It allows for highlighting passages in color as well.

It will be available on October 31 with a price tag of $279.99.

Also revealed was the new Kindle Scribe, which is the most expensive of the new models at $399.99. Shipments begin on December 4.

"The display has new white borders, and the screen has a smooth, paper-like texture that makes it look and feel like you're writing on a sheet of paper," Amazon said.

The new Scribe also has AI features.

"The all-new, built-in AI-powered notebook enables you to quickly summarize pages and pages of notes into concise bullets in a script font that can be easily shared directly from the notebook tab," Amazon noted.

The new Kindle Paperwhite is marketed as its fastest model ever, with a larger 7-inch display and up to three months of battery life. It comes in raspberry, jade and black. It also comes in a Signature Edition which has double the storage at 32 GB and metallic colors. It is available now at $159.99 and $199.99, respectively.

Lastly, Amazon introduced a new entry-level Kindle with a six-inch display and available in matcha. It is available now at $109.99.

The Kindle announcement by Amazon comes one day after Apple (AAPL) revealed its new iPad mini.

data.title: Activist Engine Capital still pursuing proxy battle with Dye & Durham
data.description:
Activist investor Engine Capital is continuing its proxy battle with Canadian software company Dye & Durham (OTCPK:DYNDF) even after the company reached an agreement to add another larger shareholder to its board.

On Tuesday, Dye & Durham (OTCPK:DYNDF) announced an agreement to give a board to seat to Blacksheep Fund Management, according to a statement. The company also said a previously called special meeting by Engine Capital has been cancelled.

“In Dye & Durham’s attempts to pull the wool over shareholders’ eyes, it finally appears to have gotten a black sheep," Engine Capital said in a statement on Wednesday. "We believe leadership’s efforts to intimidate shareholders signal its desperation to retain control of the Company. While we will keep an open mind about the Company’s new director appointments, we recall that the Board has refreshed itself numerous times before – with no meaningful progress or improvement to show for it."

Engine Capital plans to introduce its slate of directors for the Dye & Durham board in the coming weeks.

Engine Capital, which has a 7.1% stake in Dye & Durham, has been pushing for changes at the company since earlier this year. Engine Capital has argued that the company's intrinsic value is at least $25 per share.

data.title: Synchrony credit card delinquency, net charge-off rates climb in September
data.description:
• Synchrony Financial's (NYSE:SYF) credit card delinquency and net charge-off rates both increased in September, signs that inflation may be pressuring some borrowers' ability to keep up with payments.
  • 30+ day delinquency rate rose to 4.8% from 4.6% in August and compared with 4.4% in both the year-ago and five-year-ago periods.
  • Its adjusted net charge-off rate of 6.2% jumped from 5.7% in August, which had dipped from 6.3% in July. The September 2024 reading compared with 4.4% in September 2023 and 5.3% in September 2019.
  • Like many other credit card lenders, borrowing activity moderated in September, with period-end loan receivables of $102.2B slipping 0.3% M/M and rising 4.4% Y/Y.

data.title: Yum China is one of the consumer sector stocks tipped to see election volatility
data.description:
Yum China Holdings (NYSE:YUMC) is one of the stocks in the consumer sector that is seen sensitive to the outcome of the U.S. election.

Analysts have warned that if election results create escalating geopolitical concerns, it could lead to short-term fund outflow and changes to China's stimulus policies. For its part, Bank of America said Yum China (YUMC) could arguably be one of the most popular China consumer proxies for U.S. long-only investors. "We view any pullback as a particularly attractive opportunity," noted analyst Chen Luo on the potential for November share price swings.

Yum China (YUMC) is expected to report Q3 earnings in the next few weeks. Consensus estimates are for revenue of $3.02 billion, same-store sales growth of -2%, and EPS of $0.65 to be disclosed. Yum China (YUMC) has beat EPS estimates in three straight quarters, but missed on the revenue line in four of the last five reports.

Shares of Yum China (YUMC) are up 25.1% over the last six weeks as the China stimulus developments brought in more buyers. YUMC trades above its 100-day and 200-day moving averages. The restaurant stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating from Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street analysts.

data.title: Microsoft-backed Mistral AI unveils new AI models
data.description:
• Mistral AI, a generative artificial intelligence startup that has a partnership with Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), unveiled two new AI models on Wednesday: Ministral 3B and Ministral 8B.
  • Ministral 3B and Ministral 8B (collectively known as les Ministraux) are models that are designed for edge devices, such as phones and laptops, as opposed to traditional large language models, which are run on servers.
  • "Our most innovative customers and partners have increasingly been asking for local, privacy-first inference for critical applications such as on-device translation, internet-less smart assistants, local analytics, and autonomous robotics," Mistral wrote on the company blog. "Les Ministraux were built to provide a compute-efficient and low-latency solution for these scenarios. From independent hobbyists to global manufacturing teams, les Ministraux deliver for a wide variety of use cases."
  • Both models have context lengths of 128,000 tokens, the company added.
  • The models are available for download presently, but only for research purposes. Mistral said the models would be available from its cloud partners for commercial use "shortly."

data.title: Vale says quarterly iron ore production highest since 2018
data.description:
Vale (NYSE:VALE) +1.1% in Wednesday's trading after reporting its highest iron ore production in nearly six years during Q3, rising 5.5% to 91M metric tons for its best quarterly total since Q4 2018.

The company said the gains were the result of improved performance at a trio of Brazilian mining projects: S11D, Itabira and Brucutu.

Vale (VALE) said the Q3 average realized price of its iron ore fines fell nearly 14% Y/Y and 7.7% Q/Q to ~$91/ton in the quarter.

Q3 copper production increased 5% Y/Y to 85,900 tons, reflecting stronger performance across all operations, and nickel output jumped nearly 12% Y/Y to 47,100 tons, due to stronger performance at the Sudbury project and the ramp-up of Voisey's Bay underground mines, both in Canada.

Analysts at RBC Capital said Vale's (VALE) production results came in above expectations across the entire portfolio and the company is on track to achieve its full-year guidance.

Last month, Vale (VALE) raised its 2024 iron ore forecast to 323M-330M tons after a stronger than expected H1, which the company attributed to continued improvements on operational stability and asset reliability.

While Vale's (VALE) Q3 sales volumes lagged production, the company expects reduced inventory build at the iron ore division in Q4, which RBC analysts said should translate into an upgrade of market estimates for 2024 core earnings.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P seesaw, Dow gains slightly as investors digest earnings after tech rout
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were largely muted on Wednesday, a day after chip stocks slumped on a disappointing outlook from ASML (ASML).

The S&P 500 (SP500) fluctuated in morning trade, last +0.1%. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) also wavered, last -0.1%, while the Dow (DJI) was +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.01%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 1 basis point to 3.94%.

Wall Street closed in the red on Tuesday, as chip stocks took it on the chin following disappointing earnings from Dutch giant ASML (ASML) and news that the U.S. was mulling further export caps.

"Some rare weakness has come through in risk over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 falling back from its record high the previous day," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

ASML (ASML) shares were down 4.8%, after the firm said it expects 2025 net sales forecast to be between €30B to €35B (mid-point of €32.5B). The Dutch company also missed its third-quarter bookings estimates.

Morgan Stanley (MS) delivered a beat on both its top and bottom line in its third quarter, as banks continued to shine.

U.S. import and export prices fell more than expected in September.

data.title: Verisk estimates Milton insured losses at up to $50B, exceeding Helen's tally
data.description:
Verisk (NASDAQ:VRSK) estimated that insured industry losses to onshore property for Hurricane Milton will range between $30B and $50B, with the majority of insured loss attributable to wind, the company said on Wednesday.

Other losses included in the estimate are storm surge damage and rain-induced flooding resulting from Milton's landfall in Florida.

Last week, Verisk (VRSK) estimated insured U.S. losses of $6B-$11B for Hurricane Helene. While the overall levels of damage between the two storms are comparable, the estimated insured losses attributable to Milton are much higher for Milton, as wind damage leads to a higher percentage of potentially insurable loss will be paid out.

Also, Helene's damage was largely caused by flooding, particularly in inland areas affected by rain-induced flooding. Many in those inland areas did not carry flood insurance.

The figure calculates industry insured losses to onshore residential, commercial, and industrial properties and automobiles for their building, contents, and time element coverage.

The Verisk loss estimate doesn't include losses paid out by the National Flood Insurance Program; losses exacerbated by litigation, fraudulent assignment of benefits, or social inflation; losses to uninsured properties; and losses for U.S. offshore assets and non-U.S. property, among other exclusions.

With the approach of Milton, P&C insurance stocks slid on Oct. 7, 2024. Since then, they have recovered the decline, as seen in the Invesco KBW Property & Casualty Insurance ETF (NASDAQ:KBWP), still its 1.4% increase in the past month lagged the S&P 500's 3.6% gain.

Relevant insurance tickers include: Allstate (NYSE:ALL), Progressive (NYSE:PGR), Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.B) (NYSE:BRK.A), Chubb (NYSE:CB), American International Group (NYSE:AIG), Travelers Companies (NYSE:TRV), and Renaisance Re Holdings (NYSE:RNR).
